title: Components
description: Holds a set of reusable objects for different aspects of the OAS.

__schema1.title: Schema Object
__schema1.description: A Schema Object defines the shape of a JSON value.

__schema2.title: Reference Object
__schema2.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

examples.description: A user is a type of person.
A pet is a type of animal.
A pet is a type of animal.

__schema3.title: Reference
__schema3.description: A reference to another component in the specification.

__schema7.title: summary
__schema7.description: A short summary which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema8.title: description
__schema8.description: A description which by default SHOULD override that of the referenced component.

__schema10.title: String Object
__schema10.description: A String Object defines the shape of a string value.

__schema11.title: title
__schema11.description: A title for the schema, used for documentation.

__schema12.title: description
__schema12.description: A description of the schema. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema13.title: default
__schema13.description: The default value for this schema.

__schema14.title: example
__schema14.description: An example value for this schema.

__schema17.title: enum
__schema17.description: An array of valid values for this schema.

__schema19.title: const
__schema19.description: A constant value that this schema must match.

__schema21.title: Discriminator
__schema21.description: When request bodies or response payloads may be one of a number of different schemas, a discriminator object can be used to aid in serialization, deserialization, and validation.

__schema22.title: propertyName
__schema22.description: The name of the property in the payload that will hold the discriminator value.

__schema23.title: mapping
__schema23.description: An object to hold mappings between payload values and schema names or references.

__schema26.title: defaultMapping
__schema26.description: The default mapping to use when no matching discriminator value is found.

__schema29.title: XML
__schema29.description: A metadata object that allows for more fine-tuned XML model definitions.

__schema30.title: name
__schema30.description: Replaces the name of the element/attribute used for the described schema property.

__schema31.title: namespace
__schema31.description: The URI of the namespace definition.

__schema32.title: prefix
__schema32.description: The prefix to be used for the name.

__schema33.description: Declares whether the property definition translates to an attribute instead of an element.
__schema33.title: attribute

__schema35.description: May be used only for an array definition. Signifies whether the array is wrapped or not.
__schema35.title: wrapped

__schema39.title: ExternalDocumentation
__schema39.description: Allows referencing an external resource for extended documentation.

__schema40.title: description
__schema40.description: A short description of the target documentation.

__schema41.title: url
__schema41.description: The URL for the target documentation.

__schema43.title: readOnly
__schema43.description: When true, the property is only returned in responses, not accepted in requests.

__schema44.title: writeOnly
__schema44.description: When true, the property is only accepted in requests, not returned in responses.

__schema45.title: deprecated
__schema45.description: When true, indicates this schema is deprecated and should be avoided.

__schema47.title: format
__schema47.description: Semantic format hint for string values. Common formats: date-time, email, uuid, uri, hostname, ipv4, byte, binary.

__schema48.title: Pattern
__schema48.description: A regular expression pattern the string must match.

__schema55.title: contentMediaType
__schema55.description: Media type of the encoded string content.

__schema58.title: Number Object
__schema58.description: A Number Object defines the shape of a number value.

__schema60.title: format
__schema60.description: Number precision format. Use 'float' for 32-bit single-precision or 'double' (default) for 64-bit double-precision IEEE 754.

__schema61.title: multipleOf
__schema61.description: Require the number to be a multiple of this value.

__schema63.title: minimum
__schema63.description: Inclusive lower bound for the number.

__schema65.title: maximum
__schema65.description: Inclusive upper bound for the number.

__schema67.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema67.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema69.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema69.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema71.title: Integer Object
__schema71.description: An Integer Object defines the shape of an integer value.

__schema73.title: format
__schema73.description: Integer size format. Use 'int32' for signed 32-bit (-2B to 2B) or 'int64' for signed 64-bit integers.

__schema74.title: multipleOf
__schema74.description: Require the integer to be a multiple of this value.

__schema76.title: minimum
__schema76.description: Inclusive lower bound for the integer.

__schema78.title: maximum
__schema78.description: Inclusive upper bound for the integer.

__schema80.title: exclusiveMinimum
__schema80.description: Exclusive lower bound (instance must be > this value).

__schema82.title: exclusiveMaximum
__schema82.description: Exclusive upper bound (instance must be < this value).

__schema84.title: Boolean Object
__schema84.description: A Boolean Object defines the shape of a boolean value.

__schema86.title: Null Object
__schema86.description: A Null Object defines the shape of a null value.

__schema88.title: Array Object
__schema88.description: An Array Object defines the shape of an array value.

__schema92.title: prefixItems
__schema92.description: Tuple validation: schemas for items by index (array position).

__schema94.title: contains
__schema94.description: Require the array to contain at least one item matching this subschema.

__schema106.title: Object Object
__schema106.description: An Object Object defines the shape of an object value.

__schema130.title: Composition Schema
__schema130.description: A schema using composition keywords (allOf, anyOf, oneOf, not, if/then/else) without a type constraint.

__schema131.title: allOf
__schema131.description: Require the instance to validate against all subschemas.

__schema133.title: anyOf
__schema133.description: Require the instance to validate against at least one subschema.

__schema135.title: oneOf
__schema135.description: Require the instance to validate against exactly one subschema.

__schema137.title: not
__schema137.description: Require the instance to NOT validate against the given subschema.

__schema139.title: if
__schema139.description: Conditional validation: if the instance matches this schema, apply then; otherwise apply else.

__schema143.title: else
__schema143.description: Subschema applied when if does not match.

__schema146.title: Response
__schema146.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema147.title: Response
__schema147.description: Describes a single response from an API Operation.

__schema151.title: Header
__schema151.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema152.title: Header
__schema152.description: The Header Object follows the structure of the Parameter Object.

__schema153.title: description
__schema153.description: A brief description of the parameter.

__schema154.description: Determines whether this parameter is mandatory.
__schema154.title: required

__schema156.description: Specifies that a parameter is deprecated.
__schema156.title: deprecated

__schema158.description: Sets the ability to pass empty-valued parameters.
__schema158.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema160.title: style
__schema160.description: Describes how the parameter value will be serialized.

__schema175.title: externalValue
__schema175.description: A URL that points to the literal example.

__schema176.title: dataValue
__schema176.description: The data value of the example before serialization. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema177.title: serializedValue
__schema177.description: The serialized representation of the example. Mutually exclusive with value and externalValue.

__schema185.title: MediaType
__schema185.description: Each Media Type Object provides schema and examples for the media type identified by its key.

__schema193.title: Encoding
__schema193.description: A single encoding definition applied to a single schema property.

__schema194.title: contentType
__schema194.description: The Content-Type for encoding a specific property.

__schema207.title: itemSchema
__schema207.description: Schema for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema208.title: itemEncoding
__schema208.description: Encoding for individual items in streaming responses.

__schema213.title: Link
__schema213.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema214.title: Link
__schema214.description: The Link object represents a possible design-time link for a response.

__schema215.title: operationId
__schema215.description: The name of an existing, resolvable OAS operation, as defined with a unique operationId.

__schema216.title: operationRef
__schema216.description: A relative or absolute URI reference to an OAS operation.

__schema217.title: parameters
__schema217.description: A map representing parameters to pass to an operation as specified with operationId or identified via operationRef.

__schema220.title: requestBody
__schema220.description: A literal value or {expression} to use as a request body when calling the target operation.

__schema223.title: Server
__schema223.description: An object representing a Server. OpenAPI 3.2 adds 'name' for identification.

__schema224.title: url
__schema224.description: A URL to the target host. Supports server variables in {braces}. May be relative.

__schema225.title: description
__schema225.description: An optional description of the server.

__schema226.title: variables
__schema226.description: A map of server variables for URL template substitution.

__schema228.title: ServerVariable
__schema228.description: A server variable for URL template substitution. Use {variableName} in the server URL.

__schema229.title: enum
__schema229.description: An enumeration of valid string values for this variable.

__schema231.title: default
__schema231.description: The default value for substitution if not supplied.

__schema232.title: description
__schema232.description: An optional description for the server variable.

__schema234.title: name
__schema234.description: A unique name to identify the server.

__schema241.title: name
__schema241.description: The name of the query parameter. Case-sensitive.

__schema243.title: description
__schema243.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema248.description: Allows empty values (?param=). Deprecated in favor of schema-level validation.
__schema248.title: allowEmptyValue

__schema251.title: explode
__schema251.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema252.description: When true, allows reserved characters without percent-encoding.
__schema252.title: allowReserved

__schema256.title: examples
__schema256.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema258.title: content
__schema258.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema262.title: name
__schema262.description: The name of the path parameter. Must match a template expression in the path.

__schema264.title: description
__schema264.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema265.description: Path parameters must always be required.
__schema265.title: required

__schema269.title: explode
__schema269.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema272.title: examples
__schema272.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema274.title: content
__schema274.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema278.title: name
__schema278.description: The name of the header. Case-insensitive.

__schema280.title: description
__schema280.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema285.title: style
__schema285.description: Serialization style. Only 'simple' is valid for headers.

__schema286.title: explode
__schema286.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema289.title: examples
__schema289.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema291.title: content
__schema291.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

__schema297.title: description
__schema297.description: A description of the parameter. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema302.title: style
__schema302.description: Serialization style. Only 'form' is valid for cookies.

__schema303.title: explode
__schema303.description: For arrays/objects. When true, each value gets its own parameter.

__schema306.title: examples
__schema306.description: Multiple named examples. Mutually exclusive with 'example'.

__schema308.title: content
__schema308.description: For complex parameters. Mutually exclusive with 'schema'.

additionalProperties.title: SecurityScheme
additionalProperties.description: Defines a security scheme that can be used by the operations.

__schema326.title: name
__schema326.description: The name of the header, query, or cookie parameter.

__schema327.title: in
__schema327.description: Location of the API key: 'header' (most common), 'query', or 'cookie'.

__schema328.title: description
__schema328.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema330.title: HttpSecurityScheme
__schema330.description: HTTP authentication security scheme (Basic, Bearer, etc.).

__schema333.title: bearerFormat
__schema333.description: Hint for bearer token format. Only applicable when scheme='bearer'.

__schema334.title: description
__schema334.description: A description of the security scheme.

__schema343.title: flows
__schema343.description: Allows configuration of the supported OAuth Flows.

__schema345.title: OAuthFlow
__schema345.description: Configuration details for a supported OAuth Flow.

__schema346.title: authorizationUrl
__schema346.description: The authorization URL to be used for this flow.

__schema347.title: tokenUrl
__schema347.description: The token URL to be used for this flow.

__schema348.title: refreshUrl
__schema348.description: The URL to be used for obtaining refresh tokens.

__schema349.title: scopes
__schema349.description: The available scopes for the OAuth2 security scheme.

__schema356.title: device
__schema356.description: Configuration for the Device Authorization Grant flow.

__schema363.title: openIdConnectUrl
__schema363.description: OpenID Connect URL to discover OAuth2 configuration values.

additionalProperties.title: Callback
additionalProperties.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema369.title: Callback
__schema369.description: A map of possible out-of-band callbacks related to the parent operation.

__schema371.title: PathItem
__schema371.description: Describes the operations available on a single path.

__schema378.title: Operation
__schema378.description: Describes a single API operation on a path.

__schema379.title: tags
__schema379.description: A list of tags for API documentation grouping.

__schema381.title: summary
__schema381.description: A short summary of the operation. Keep under ~120 characters.

__schema382.title: description
__schema382.description: A verbose description. CommonMark syntax MAY be used.

__schema383.title: operationId
__schema383.description: Unique identifier for the operation. Used for code generation.

__schema384.title: parameters
__schema384.description: Parameters for this operation, combined with path-level parameters.

__schema392.description: Marks this operation as deprecated. Defaults to false.

__schema393.title: security
__schema393.description: Security requirements for this operation. Overrides root-level security.

__schema394.title: SecurityRequirement
__schema394.description: Lists the required security schemes for this operation.

__schema409.title: additionalOperations
__schema409.description: Custom HTTP methods beyond the standard ones.
